perturbation.original_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person agrees to immediately destroy all such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board , in the following manner: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, must be destroyed by burning or shredding.
perturbation.changed_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person may, at their sole discretion, retain or destroy such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board. Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, can be archived indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity and a direct contradiction regarding the disposal of confidential information. The original text mandated immediate destruction. The revision permits Authorized Person to choose whether to retain or destroy the information. Further, it contradicts the destruction policy by stating that it 'can be archived indefinitely'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person may, at their sole discretion, retain or destroy such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board. Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, can be archived indefinitely. Printed material to be burned must be burned in an incinerator that produces enough heat to burn the entire bundle, or the bundle must be separated to ensure that all pages are incinerated.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated immediate destruction of confidential information. The changed text allows the Authorized Person to choose to retain or destroy the information, and further states that it 'can be archived indefinitely'. The contradicted text states that Printed material to be burned must be burned in an incinerator. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing indefinite archiving, nullifying the incineration requirement and introducing a strong in-text contradiction, in line with the structural flaws - in text contradiction definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person may provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, without authorization in writing by KDOL, as long as the Authorized Person deems it necessary for the project success, removing any restrictions on sharing confidential data.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits sharing confidential information with third parties without KDOL's written authorization. The modified text now allows the Authorized Person to share the information freely if they deem it necessary for project success, directly contradicting the prior prohibition. This creates a significant in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorized Person may provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, without authorization in writing by KDOL, as long as the Authorized Person deems it necessary for the project success, removing any restrictions on sharing confidential data. A copy of any such agreement shall be available for inspection upon request by the KDOL and will not be subject to disclosure to third parties by anyone other than KDOL .
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits sharing confidential information with third parties without KDOL's written authorization. The changed text allows the Authorized Person to share the information freely if they deem it necessary for project success, directly contradicting the prior prohibition. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Based on the explanation provided, the original text and the changed text contradict, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be l imited to the minimum number of individuals necessary to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be granted to all employees and contractors of the Authorized Person, regardless of their role in achieving the stated purpose of the agreement, promoting transparency within the organization, in direct conflict with the restriction that access should be minimal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates restricted access to the information. The modified version broadens access to all employees and contractors, regardless of their need-to-know, contradicting the limitation. This introduces in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties shall exercise reas onable and prudent security procedures to protect such information, reports, returns and other documentation in their possession, including electronic versions thereof, from any unauthorized access and/or disclosure. Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be granted to all employees and contractors of the Authorized Person, regardless of their role in achieving the stated purpose of the agreement, promoting transparency within the organization, in direct conflict with the restriction that access should be minimal. Notwithstanding any provisions of this agreement , any breach of the confidentiality or non- disclosure provisions above shall be grounds for immediate termination of this agreement without notice, and legal action may be immediately commenced.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited access to information, whereas the changed text broadens access to all employees and contractors regardless of their role. The contradicted text requires 'reasonable and prudent security procedures to protect such information...from any unauthorized access'. Granting access to all employees and contractors regardless of need directly conflicts with limiting unauthorized access and protecting information, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential i nformation provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom, shall not be disclosed to any individual(s) that is/are not subject to this agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential information provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom, may be disclosed to individuals not subject to this agreement, if the Authorized Person deems it beneficial for project collaboration. This directly contradicts the original statement prohibiting disclosure to non-subject individuals, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential i nformation provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom, may be disclosed to individuals not subject to this agreement, if the Authorized Person deems it beneficial for project collaboration. This directly contradicts the original statement prohibiting disclosure to non-subject individuals, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity. Information provided hereunder, or identifiable information derived therefrom , shall not be copied, downloaded or otherwise placed in any format, including working papers, which may become a public record pursuant to the Kansas Open Records Act (KORA).
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidential information 'shall not be disclosed' to individuals not subject to the agreement. The changed text states that confidential information 'may be disclosed' to individuals not subject to the agreement. This is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text also says information 'shall not be copied, downloaded or otherwise placed in any format', thus reinforcing the initial 'shall not be disclosed' statement and contrasting the changed text. The perturbation explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction and ambiguity, aligning with the definition of structural flaws causing in-text contradictions.
